stories.title: The fanfiction community is at war with AI — and itself
stories.simple_summary: Fanfiction writers are fighting against other fanfiction writers who use AI to write stories. The methods people are using to catch AI writers might accidentally catch regular writers too.
stories.why_it_matters: If you write fanfiction as a hobby, you could get accused unfairly — but if you just read fanfiction, this doesn't affect you.

stories.title: The only AI glossary you’ll need this year
stories.simple_summary: TechCrunch published a list explaining all the new AI words and phrases that people keep using.
stories.why_it_matters: This is just a reference tool — helpful if you want to understand the words you're hearing, but not something you need to do anything about.

stories.title: The browser wars aren’t about search anymore — here are the best alternatives to Chrome and Safari
stories.simple_summary: This article talks about different web browsers you can use instead of Chrome or Safari (the programs you use to look at websites).
stories.why_it_matters: This is mainly for people interested in trying different browser options — nothing urgent for your everyday use.

stories.title: Anthropic wants to develop its own drugs
stories.simple_summary: Anthropic, an AI company, announced a new tool called Claude Science to help scientists organize their research and create charts and diagrams.
stories.why_it_matters: This only matters if you're a scientist or involved in medical research — regular business owners won't use this.

stories.title: A behind-the-scenes look at Midjourney’s medical scanner leaves many questions unanswered
stories.simple_summary: An AI company called Midjourney showed a video of an ultrasound machine they're developing that uses AI, but they haven't proven yet that it actually works well.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly hype right now — the machine isn't available yet and may never work as promised, so there's nothing for you to act on.

stories.title: Mark Zuckerberg tells staff that AI agents haven’t progressed as quickly as he’d hoped
stories.simple_summary: Mark Zuckerberg told his employees that AI progress at Meta is moving slower than he expected.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors — it's nothing that affects your day-to-day business.

stories.title: Jersey Mike’s IPO illustrates how bad the AI hype has become
stories.simple_summary: A sandwich shop called Jersey Mike's mentioned AI in their documents when they went public (sold stock to investors), even though AI has nothing to do with their business.
stories.why_it_matters: This shows companies are just adding AI into their plans to sound trendy — if you see an AI mention that doesn't make sense, it's probably just hype.

stories.title: Meta quietly launches vibe-coded gaming app Pocket
stories.simple_summary: Meta (Facebook's parent company) quietly released an app where you can type descriptions and have AI create small games for you to play.
stories.why_it_matters: This is an experimental game app — only relevant if you like trying out new gaming apps from Meta.

stories.title: Anthropic is discussing a new custom chip with Samsung
stories.simple_summary: Anthropic is talking to Samsung about building a special computer chip designed just for AI.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly for investors and engineers — it doesn't change anything for regular business owners.

stories.title: OpenAI proposed donating 5% of its equity to a US sovereign wealth fund
stories.simple_summary: The CEO of OpenAI suggested giving 5% of his company to the U.S. government to let regular people share in the profits from AI.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a discussion about how to split money from AI companies with the public — mostly relevant to investors and policy people.

stories.title: Microsoft launches its own AI deployment company with $2.5 billion commitment
stories.simple_summary: Microsoft announced a new division with $2.5 billion to help companies set up and use AI systems.
stories.why_it_matters: If you run a business and Microsoft approaches you about AI services, this explains they're now offering that — but you'd need to decide if it helps your business.

stories.title: Yep, we’re using OpenClaw to date now
stories.simple_summary: Someone created an automated script using AI tools to message many women on Instagram for dating purposes.
stories.why_it_matters: This is an example of AI being used in a way that bothers people — nothing you need to worry about unless you're dating online.
